title: CSS Introduction
content:
### What is CSS?

**CSS** stands for **Cascading Style Sheets**. It describes how HTML elements should be displayed on screen, paper, or other media.

---

### What Can CSS Do?

| Capability | Example |
|-----------|---------|
| **Colors** | Text, backgrounds, borders |
| **Layout** | Flexbox, Grid, positioning |
| **Typography** | Fonts, sizes, spacing, shadows |
| **Animations** | Transitions, keyframes |
| **Responsive** | Media queries, flexible layouts |

---

### CSS Saves Work

Instead of styling every HTML element individually, CSS defines styles **once** and applies them across multiple pages.

```css
h1 {
  color: blue;
  font-size: 32px;
}
```

---

### CSS Timeline

| Version | Year | Key Features |
|---------|------|-------------|
| CSS1 | 1996 | Fonts, colors, margins |
| CSS2 | 1998 | Positioning, z-index |
| CSS3 | 2012+ | Flexbox, Grid, animations, media queries |

---

> **Key Point:** CSS is a **style sheet language** that controls the presentation of HTML documents. It is not a programming language.
